title: Agent Guardrails
competency: 01. Toolchain Setup - Project rules, hooks, guardrails, and CLI or MCP wiring
mission: Create safety guardrails for a coding agent before allowing it to work independently on a repository.
domain: Internal release workflow console with simulated secret files, production configuration, generated clients, database migrations, and legacy workflows.
outcome: A coding agent can make normal application changes while avoiding secrets, generated artifacts, production files, dangerous commands, and approval-gated workflows.
seededDefects: The repository includes simulated sensitive files without agent access boundaries.
Production deployment files sit beside normal application code.
Generated client code can be mistaken for editable source.
Legacy workflow commands are powerful and need approval rules.
verificationGates: .agent/guardrails.md explains safe workflows and approval points.
.agent/deny-rules.md lists forbidden commands and forbidden file operations.
Normal development commands remain available to the agent.
agentWorkflow: Inventory files and folders without reading simulated secret contents.
Classify safe application code, restricted production files, generated code, and migration history.
Write guardrails that preserve normal edit/test workflows.
Document human approval requirements before destructive or production-impacting work.
workingDeliverables: Evidence that normal tests still run
